title: Qubit routing LightSABRE benchmark
summary: A deterministic qubit-routing policy is replayed against a frozen 24-circuit LightSABRE portfolio across Q20, Willow, and Heron-FEZ topology targets. The accepted candidate reduces aggregate added CNOTs by 12,294 while keeping the topology split and case-level readout public.
problem_statement: Find a deterministic routing policy that maps logical two-qubit interactions onto nearest-neighbor hardware coupling graphs while reducing added CNOT count against the fixed LightSABRE reference.

evaluation_contract.summary: Candidates are evaluated on 24 benchmark circuits crossed with Q20, Willow, and Heron-FEZ coupling graphs. The evaluator compares added CNOT count against LightSABRE, uses a lower-is-better negative weighted CNOT-reduction score, and requires deterministic replay validity across all 72 cases.

website.card_summary: A routing policy is replayed against LightSABRE on a frozen 72-case portfolio across Q20, Willow, and Heron-FEZ targets.

figure_captions.assets/objective-curve.svg: Objective trace for the curated public chain. Faint points are scored candidates, the solid line is retained best-so-far weighted CNOT reduction, and rings mark baseline and accepted.
figure_captions.assets/target-comparison.svg: Per-topology added-CNOT comparison for Q20, Willow, and Heron-FEZ. Shorter accepted bars mean fewer added CNOTs than the LightSABRE reference.
figure_captions.assets/routing-readout.svg: Selected largest case-level savings from the replay export, sorted by absolute CNOT reduction versus LightSABRE.

website.score_note: The governed score is the negative weighted CNOT reduction versus LightSABRE, so lower is better. The report also shows positive CNOT reductions because that is the more readable routing-quality readout.
